title: Hong Kong 14-Day City Break
metaTitle: Hong Kong 14-Day City Break | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 14-day trip to Hong Kong, Hong Kong SAR: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Victoria Peak Tram & Sky Terrace and Hong Kong Disneyland. Prices from $2644 per person (estimate).
overview: Spend 14 days discovering Hong Kong, Hong Kong SAR — Subtropical climate with hot, humid summers (May-Sep) including typhoon risk, and mild, dry winters (Dec-Feb); spring brings fog and drizzle. October to December for dry, mild, sunny weather with lower humidity. This itinerary blends Victoria Peak Tram & Sky Terrace, Hong Kong Disneyland, Star Ferry with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
13 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Victoria Peak Tram & Sky Terrace, Hong Kong Disneyland, Star Ferry
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Hong Kong
itinerary.description: Land at Hong Kong International Airport (HKG) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Victoria Peak Tram & Sky Terrace — Iconic funicular and skyline viewpoint. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Hong Kong: Hong Kong Disneyland & Ocean Park
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Hong Kong Disneyland — Compact Disney theme park. In the afternoon, continue to Ocean Park — Marine-life theme park with rides. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Hong Kong: Ngong Ping 360 Cable Car & Hong Kong Museum of History
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Ngong Ping 360 Cable Car — Cable car to the Big Buddha at Lantau Island. In the afternoon, continue to Hong Kong Museum of History — Comprehensive city and regional history museum. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Lantau Island and Big Buddha
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Lantau Island and Big Buddha, a popular side trip from Hong Kong. Return to Hong Kong in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Lantau Island and Big Buddha

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Cheung Chau Island — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Hong Kong: M+ Museum & Hong Kong Palace Museum
itinerary.description: Morning visit to M+ Museum — Museum of visual culture in West Kowloon. In the afternoon, continue to Hong Kong Palace Museum — Chinese imperial art and artifacts. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Macau
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Macau, a popular side trip from Hong Kong. Return to Hong Kong in the evening with time free to explore on your own.

itinerary.title: Hong Kong: Symphony of Lights Harbour Cruise & Madame Tussauds Hong Kong
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Symphony of Lights Harbour Cruise — Evening light-show cruise on Victoria Harbour. In the afternoon, continue to Madame Tussauds Hong Kong — Wax figure museum atop Victoria Peak. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Sai Kung Global Geopark boat tour — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.
activities: Sai Kung Global Geopark boat tour

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Cheung Chau Island
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Cheung Chau Island, a popular side trip from Hong Kong. Return to Hong Kong in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Cheung Chau Island

itinerary.title: Hong Kong: Hong Kong Wetland Park & Star Ferry
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Hong Kong Wetland Park — Nature reserve and eco-education center. In the afternoon, continue to Star Ferry — Historic harbour crossing with skyline views. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Hong Kong: Avenue of Stars & Man Mo Temple
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Avenue of Stars — Waterfront promenade honoring Hong Kong cinema. In the afternoon, continue to Man Mo Temple — Historic incense-filled Taoist temple. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Blue House Cluster Wan Chai — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Temple Street Night Market or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to HKG for your departure flight.

travelTips: Get an Octopus card on arrival for all public transport
Book Michelin-starred dim sum spots online in advance
Avoid Golden Week and Lunar New Year for crowds and price spikes
Use the free Star Ferry for the best skyline photos
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Light breathable clothing for humid summers
A light jacket for cooler winter evenings
Compact umbrella year-round for sudden showers
Comfortable shoes for hilly, escalator-heavy walking
thingsToAvoid: Tailor scams in Tsim Sha Tsui promising unrealistically cheap suits
Standing on the left on escalators
Overpaying at unmetered taxis near tourist zones
Visiting outlying islands during typhoon signal warnings
topRestaurants: Tim Ho Wan — Cantonese dim sum ($)
Lin Heung Tea House — Traditional dim sum ($)
Tai Hing Roast — Cantonese roast meats ($)
Yat Lok Roast Goose — Cantonese roast goose ($$)
Kau Kee Restaurant — Beef brisket noodles ($)
Australia Dairy Company — Hong Kong-style cha chaan teng ($)
instagramSpots: Symphony of Lights from Avenue of Stars
hiddenGems: Sai Kung Global Geopark boat tour
familyActivities: Ngong Ping cable car and village
luxuryExperiences: Suite stay at The Peninsula Hong Kong
Private junk boat charter around outlying islands
Michelin tasting menu at Lung King Heen
Private tailor-made suit fitting in Central
honeymoonExperiences: Couples spa at a Peak hotel
budgetExperiences: Temple Street Night Market street food
localEtiquette: Queue in an orderly line for public transport and escalators; stand on the right.
Use both hands when giving or receiving business cards or gifts.
It is polite to leave a small amount of change rather than exact-tipping in casual eateries.
Avoid discussing politics with locals unless they raise it themselves.
Remove shoes when entering someone's home.
localTransportGuide: The MTR subway is fast, clean, and covers nearly all attractions; get an Octopus card for trains, buses, ferries, and even convenience stores. Taxis are metered and inexpensive for short hops; the Star Ferry remains the cheapest scenic harbour crossing.
bestTimeToVisit: October to December for dry, mild, sunny weather with lower humidity.
weather: Subtropical climate with hot, humid summers (May-Sep) including typhoon risk, and mild, dry winters (Dec-Feb); spring brings fog and drizzle.
visaInformation: Most Western passport holders (US, UK, EU, Canada, Australia) can enter visa-free for 90-180 days for tourism. No advance e-visa is needed for these nationalities; other nationalities should check requirements as Hong Kong maintains its own immigration policy separate from mainland China.
averageFlightTime: ~10.7h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Hong Kong?
faqs.answer: Most Western passport holders (US, UK, EU, Canada, Australia) can enter visa-free for 90-180 days for tourism. No advance e-visa is needed for these nationalities; other nationalities should check requirements as Hong Kong maintains its own immigration policy separate from mainland China.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Hong Kong?
faqs.answer: October to December for dry, mild, sunny weather with lower humidity. Subtropical climate with hot, humid summers (May-Sep) including typhoon risk, and mild, dry winters (Dec-Feb); spring brings fog and drizzle.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Hong Kong safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Hong Kong is rated very safe for tourists, with low rates of violent crime. Usual travel precautions (watch belongings in crowds, use licensed taxis) still apply.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Hong Kong?
faqs.answer: The local currency is HKD. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: Marco Cohen
reviews.title: So many hidden gems included
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Symphony of Lights Harbour Cruise and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Sarah Andersen
reviews.title: Luxury without the hassle
reviews.body: Hong Kong is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Hong Kong Palace Museum. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Isabella Johnson
reviews.title: Beautiful destination, well organized
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Central-Mid-Levels Escalator and Hong Kong Museum of History. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Emma Brown
reviews.title: Unforgettable trip!
reviews.body: Our trip to Hong Kong was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Mong Kok street markets was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.
